system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

vertices

    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(

    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1b: Connection bend to outlet
    hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
            (4 8 9 5)
            (8 11 10 9)
            (11 12 13 10)
            (12 16 17 13)

);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

method          scotch;

{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_B_20260516_194747_run3/runs/squareBend/1/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 46.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 0 is "inside-out"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

**Diagnosis:**

The first block uses vertex indices `(0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)`. Looking at the vertex definitions:

- Vertex 0: `(-50, 25, 25)`
- Vertex 4: `(0, 25, 25)`
- Vertex 5: `(0, 25, -25)`
- Vertex 1: `(-50, 25, -25)` ← These four form the "bottom" face
- Vertex 3: `(-50, -25, 25)`
- Vertex 7: `(0, -25, 25)`
- Vertex 6: `(0, -25, -25)`
- Vertex 2: `(-50, -25, -25)` ← These four form the "top" face

For a hex block, the first four vertices should define one face and the second four should define the opposite face, with the convention that vertex i and vertex i+4 are connected. The problem is that **the winding order of the bottom face `(0 4 5 1)` is clockwise when viewed from outside**, making the block inside-out.

**Fix:** Reverse the winding order of each face in the hex definition so that the normal of the first face points outward (right-hand rule). For Block 0, change:
```
hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)
```
to:
```
hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)
```

Alternatively, more systematically, examine the geometry: the bottom face should have vertices ordered so that looking from outside the domain toward the inside, the ordering is counterclockwise. The correct ordering for the bottom face is `(0 → 1 → 5 → 4)` and top face is `(3 → 2 → 6 → 7)`, giving:
```
hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)
```

Apply the same logic to check Blocks 1, 1b, and 2. After inspection, **Blocks 1 and 1b also need their vertex orderings checked/reversed**. Specifically:

- **Block 1**: `hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)` → Change to `hex (7 3 11 9 6 2 10 8)`
- **Block 1b**: `hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)` → Change to `hex (11 3 15 10 12 2 14 13)`

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points" file

This is a cascading error — since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files. Once blockMesh runs successfully, this will be resolved automatically.

## Additional Issue: Boundary Name Mismatches

The `blockMeshDict` defines boundaries: `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`.

However, the `0/` BC files (`T`, `U`, `alphat`, `epsilon`, `k`, `nut`, `p`) reference boundaries that don't exist in the mesh:
- `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `front`, `back`, `Default_Boundary_Region`

**Fix:** Update all `0/` BC files to use only the six patches defined in `blockMeshDict`: `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`. Remove references to non-existent patches and add entries for any missing ones.

Specifically:
- **`T`**: Replace `defaultFaces` with `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` (all `zeroGradient`), and keep `inlet`/`outlet`.
- **`U`**: Replace `front`/`back` with `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` (all `noSlip`). Keep `inlet`/`outlet`.
- **`alphat`**: Replace `innerWall`/`outerWall` with `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall` using `compressible::alphatWallFunction`. Add `innerWall` if needed.
- **`epsilon`**, **`k`**, **`nut`**, **`p`**: Similarly update all non-existent patch names to match the six defined patches.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change Block 0 hex definition from hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) to hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7); Change Block 1 hex definition from hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) to hex (7 3 11 9 6 2 10 8); Change Block 1b hex definition from hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2) to hex (11 3 15 10 12 2 14 13); Replace defaultFaces boundary in T file with upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall all set to zeroGradient; Replace front and back boundaries in U file with upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall all set to noSlip; Replace innerWall and outerWall in alphat with upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall using compressible::alphatWallFunction; Replace Default_Boundary_Region in nut with inlet and outlet both using nutkWallFunction; Replace innerWall, outerWall, front, back patch references in all 0/ files with only the six valid patch names: inlet, outlet, upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
